name: htmx-skill
description: Documentation for the htmx JavaScript library (attributes, events, headers, API, extensions, examples, migration guides, server examples). Use when you need accurate htmx syntax/behavior or to locate the right reference page for an htmx question.
capabilities: Look up htmx attribute documentation with syntax and examples
Find event documentation including timing and detail properties
Reference response headers for client-side redirects and triggers
Access extension documentation for SSE, WebSockets, and more
Locate working examples for common patterns like modals and infinite scroll
author: DrZoot
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation/reference skill containing markdown files and a build script. The 2454 static findings are 99%+ false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code formatting. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are regex false positives from documentation text. Network/filesystem operations are for downloading documentation from GitHub. No malicious patterns detected.
valueStatement: Need accurate htmx syntax and behavior documentation. This skill provides comprehensive reference for htmx attributes, events, API, extensions, and examples to help you build modern web applications.
limitations: Does not execute htmx code or provide interactive examples
Does not install or configure htmx in your project
Does not provide server-side implementation code
Does not debug htmx issues or runtime errors

useCases.title: Add dynamic behavior
useCases.description: Look up htmx attributes to add AJAX functionality to HTML elements without writing JavaScript.

useCases.title: Build reactive UIs
useCases.description: Reference response headers to implement server-driven updates, redirects, and event triggers.

useCases.title: Integrate extensions
useCases.description: Find documentation for SSE and WebSocket extensions to add real-time features to your application.

promptTemplates.title: Attribute syntax
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use hx-post to submit a form and swap the response into a div?

promptTemplates.title: Event handling
promptTemplates.prompt: What events fire during an htmx request and what properties do they include?

promptTemplates.title: Response headers
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use HX-Location to redirect without a full page reload?

promptTemplates.title: Extension usage
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to use the SSE extension to update content from server-sent events.

bestPractices: Start with the most specific page for your question - prefer attribute documentation over general guides.
Open the referenced file to confirm details before implementing, as descriptions may be brief.
Check api.md for JavaScript API methods when you need programmatic control over htmx.
antiPatterns: Do not use htmx for complex client-side state management - it is designed for server-driven UIs.
Do not forget to handle validation errors - use hx-validate with HTML5 validation API.
Do not overlook hx-swap timing options - improper swap settings can cause flickering or lost focus.

faq.question: What is htmx compatible with?
faq.answer: htmx works with any server-side framework and requires no build tools. It degrades gracefully when JavaScript is disabled.

faq.question: What are htmx request limits?
faq.answer: htmx makes standard HTTP requests. Limits depend on your server configuration and network settings.

faq.question: Can htmx integrate with other libraries?
faq.answer: Yes, htmx has extensions for many use cases and can work alongside React, Vue, or other frameworks for specific components.

faq.question: Is data safe with htmx?
faq.answer: htmx uses standard browser security features. Sensitive data should not be stored in client-side HTML without protection.

faq.question: Why is my htmx request not working?
faq.answer: Check browser console for errors, verify the URL returns valid HTML, and ensure hx-target selects the correct element.

faq.question: How does htmx compare to fetch API?
faq.answer: htmx provides declarative AJAX via HTML attributes. fetch API requires JavaScript code. Use htmx for server-driven UIs and fetch for complex client logic.
